# Bits&Bytes - India's Teen-Led Code Club

> **Mission:** Getting ambitious teenagers to ship meaningful tech through hackathons, design/dev squads, and product launches. Fully student-led.
> **Org Type:** Nonprofit · Teen-Led Code Club · Based in Lucknow, India · Serving builders nationwide
> **Website:** https://gobitsnbytes.org

This file provides context for AI agents and LLM-powered search engines about Bits&Bytes.

## Quick Facts
- **Founded:** 2024
- **Location:** Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, India
- **Members:** 1500+ active student developers from across India
- **Projects Shipped:** 130+ real products (apps, web, AI)
- **Partner Schools:** 100+ and growing
- **Membership Cost:** Free. Always student-led.
- **Age Range:** 13-19 years old

## Core Identity & Culture
Bits&Bytes is a teen-led code club. Three co-founders, no adults in the room. Built from scratch. We're not a chapter of someone else's thing. We're fully independent, fully student-led, and we run events on our own terms.

We were hosting Daydream Lucknow under Hack Club. Everything was set: venue, sponsors, participants. At the last moment, Hack Club pulled the venue. So we went fully independent and built the entire hackathon in 13 days flat. That's probably the most honest thing we can tell you about us. We don't quit, and we don't make excuses.

- **Ship or dip:** Talking about your idea is easy. We'd rather have a working prototype by Sunday than a perfect slide deck by next month.
- **Your squad keeps you honest:** Mentors, pods, and the kind of peer pressure that makes you actually finish things. Nobody ghosts a project when their team is waiting on their code.
- **Built for users, not grades:** School operations, civic tech, accessibility tools. The things we ship get used by real people, not just submitted for a rubric.

## What We Do
1. **Hackathons**: Organizing events like Scrapyard Lucknow where 40+ coders tackle civic, education, and sustainability problems.
2. **Workshops**: Dev tools and AI, one weekend at a time.
3. **Projects**: Members ship real products with mentorship at every step, from idea to deployment.
4. **Community**: A safe, inclusive community where people can actually build things without garbage getting in the way.

## Current & Upcoming Events
- **India Innovates 2026** [ARCHIVED] — Held on March 28, 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi (9 AM to 7 PM). Verified scale: 1.26 crore+ applicants, narrowed to 28,000+, then 5,000+, and finally 15 teams. Domains: Urban Solutions, Digital Democracy, and Open Innovation. Prize pool: ₹10 Lakh+ with ₹1L/₹75K/₹50K/₹25K per domain. Bits&Bytes served as the Official Executive Partner. Attended by Delhi CM Rekha Gupta, Bihar Assembly Speaker, and MP Manoj Tiwari. Post-event flow included ministry-level presentation stage. Press coverage: #IndiaInnovates2026 trended on X. Details: https://indiainnovates.org

- **Execron 1.0** [ARCHIVED] — AI Hackathon & Workshop for Classes 9–12, held March 19–22, 2026 at IIT Kanpur. In collaboration with TechKriti '26. A 4-hour hands-on workshop followed by a 24-hour hackathon sprint. Topics: AI & ML, Web Development, App Development, Cybersecurity, Cloud Computing. Team sizes 1–4 members. Partners: ByteForge, Bits&Bytes. Details: https://byteforge.paxus.in/

- **GitHub Copilot Dev Days | Lucknow** [ARCHIVED] — Community developer event on April 19, 2026 at Cubispace, Jankipuram, Lucknow. Hosted by Bits&Bytes with community partners Coding Connoisseurs, Aryan Singh, and Notion Lucknow. Topics: how GitHub Copilot works, integrating AI into dev workflows, prompt techniques, and using AI responsibly. Archive link: https://luma.com/xtxua1jl.

- **Lucknow Build Guild** [ARCHIVED] — Free hardware workshop and meetup held on April 19, 2026 at SureStay by Best Western, Lucknow. Hosted by Shaurya. Event site: https://www.lucknow-build-guild.xyz/, Host: https://linktr.ee/shauryaashu and https://github.com/Shaurya-Ashu.

## Leadership Team (Core Team)
- **Yash Singh** — Co-Founder & Organisation Lead. Builds things that matter: a VS Code extension with thousands of users, hackathons with 400+ participants. IOQM National Qualifier. Built AI projects including laser pushup detection systems and PDF scrapers. Organized Scrapyard LKO and GDS MUN. Educator at STEMist Lucknow. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yash-vardhan-singh-a41540270/, GitHub: https://github.com/yashclouded, Website: https://yashvibe.codes/

- **Aadrika Maurya** — Co-Founder & Chief Creative Strategist. RSI India Alumni who conducted neuroscience research on EEG signals and attention modeling, the kind of work most people don't touch until grad school. Owns the creative direction and the strategy for what the network becomes. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/aadrika-maurya/, GitHub: https://github.com/Aadrika08, Website: https://aadrikasportfolio.framer.website/

- **Akshat Kushwaha** — Co-Founder & Technical Lead. AI-native systems engineer who asks what happens when software fails. Ex Jr. Research Engineer at jhana.ai at 17, where he built production AI systems including a Steno dictation plugin and AI-era IVRS. Owns the entire tech stack and anything that needs to work under pressure. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/akshat-singh-kushwaha/, GitHub: https://github.com/a3ro-dev, Website: https://a3ro.dev

- **Devaansh Pathak** — Founding Member & Backend Lead. Handles backend architecture and the economics of partnerships. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/devaanshpa/

- **Maryam Fatima** — Social Media & Promotions Head. Runs social strategy. 10k+ impressions on club events. Designs visual campaigns for independent hackathons. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/maryam-fatima-9719aa377/

- **Sristhi Singh** — Operations & Communications Head. Keeps 100+ members on the same page. Coordinates between design/dev squads and handles logistics for city-wide events. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/srishti-singh-ab6a1b391

## Technology Stack
- **Frontend**: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Three.js / WebGL
- **Backend**: Node.js, Python, PostgreSQL, cloud infrastructure
- **Tools**: GitHub, Discord, Vercel, Notion

## How to Join & General FAQ
- **Applying**: Visit https://gobitsnbytes.org/join. Free to join. Tech education shouldn't cost money.
- **Hackathons**: Either way works. Show up with a team or form one at the event. Solo is fine but most people have more fun on a team.
- **Hackathon Rules**: Make anything you want. Games, apps, hardware, websites. No bringing existing completed projects. Must follow the Code of Conduct.
- **Hardware/Logistics**: Bring a laptop. For overnight: toothbrush, sleeping bag, pillow. Parents can attend kickoff and awards only, unless volunteering with a background check.

## Code of Conduct (Vibe & Boundaries)
- **Values**: Be warm & welcoming, patient & chill, respectful, and build together.
- **Zero Tolerance**: Harassment, bullying, unwanted advances, explicit content, spam, misusing funds, doxxing.
- **Consequences (3 Strikes)**:
  1. Warning/apology
  2. Temporary ban
  3. Permanent removal
- **Reporting**: email `hello@gobitsnbytes.org` or message a team member. 100% confidential. Applies to all events, online spaces, projects, and social media.

## Website Structure
- **/** - Homepage with overview and impact stats
- **/about** - Team profiles and mission details
- **/events** - Upcoming and archived hackathons and workshops
- **/impact** - Community statistics and success stories
- **/join** - Free membership application
- **/contact** - Get in touch with the team
- **/faq** - Frequently asked questions
- **/coc** - Code of conduct and community guidelines
- **/qna** - AI-powered Q&A assistant

- **Instagram**: https://www.instagram.com/bitsnbytes.lko
- **Location**: Lucknow, India

## Brand Kit

### Brand Mark
- The Bits&Bytes brand mark is a compact 3D cube monogram with a small star accent. Use the full wordmark for hero headings and the cube mark (square) for avatars, favicons, and constrained spaces. Prefer the reversed white mark on dark backgrounds and the single-color mark on light backgrounds. Maintain clear space around the mark equal to the cube's height.

### Fonts & Typography
- **Primary:** Helvetica Now — headings and display; bold weights for impact.
- **Secondary:** Georgia Pro — body text and longer reading.
- **Accent (script):** Palm Club — reserved for decorative hero treatments.
- **Accent (display):** Anton — condensed/impact accents, use sparingly.

### Color System
- **Burgundy (Core gradient):**
  - Base: #97192C
  - 20%: #791423
  - 40%: #5B0F1A
  - 60%: #3C0A12
  - 80%: #1E0509
- **Neutrals (dark → light):**
  - #120F0A, #413F3B, #716F6C, #A09F9D, #D0CFCE
- **Accent — Orange (pop gradient):**
  - #FC920D, #FDA83D, #FDBE6E, #FED39E, #FEE9CF
- **Warm Accent Range (supporting):**
  - #C94218, #D46846, #DF8E74, #E9B3A3, #F4D9D1
- **Utility / Interaction colors:**
  - Vibrant Pink (Interactive): #E45A92
  - Rich Plum (Secondary): #5D2F77
  - Deep Purple (Brand accent): #3E1E68

Design guidance:
- **Burgundy / Core:** the voice of the brand — serious and composed; use as the structural anchor for hero surfaces and heavy weights.
- **Orange / Pop:** use for emphasis, micro-animations, and callouts where things should pop.
- **Gradients:** prefer layered gradients (Burgundy → Plum → Vibrant Pink → Orange) for hero and promotional areas to create depth and movement.
- **Neutrals:** use the neutral scale to layer content and preserve legibility; prefer warm neutrals when overlaying textures.

### Visual Elements
- Use rich gradients that blend Vibrant Pink → Rich Plum → Deep Purple for hero backgrounds and promotional graphics.
- Halftone and stipple textures, geometric checks, and a small star/sparkle motif are approved decorative elements. Apply these as subtle overlays or masks (low opacity) to add tactile texture.
- Maintain restrained use of script accents; keep body copy in Georgia Pro for legibility.

### Logo files & usage
- Prefer vector SVG for web and print. Provide PNG/JPEG fallbacks for legacy uses.
- Recommended assets: full wordmark, cube mark (square), monochrome (black/white) variants, and favicon-sized versions.
- Do not rotate, skew, or recolor the mark outside the defined palette. Preserve legibility and clear space at all sizes.

## AI / LLMs used by the site
- **Primary chat model (server):** google/gemini-3-flash-preview (used via Hack Club proxy)
- **Fallback chat model:** google/gemini-2.5-flash
- **Embedding model (RAG):** openai/text-embedding-3-small (used by `scripts/embed-site.ts` and `lib/rag.ts`)
- **Image generation:** google/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview (proxy); optional `stable-diffusion-3` via NVIDIA when `NVIDIA_KEY` is configured

Notes:
- All AI API calls use the `HACKCLUB_PROXY_API_KEY` environment variable and the Hack Club proxy endpoints.
- The assistant also relies on Supabase environment variables (`NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`) for session persistence.

## RAG / Indexing (maintenance)
- The site uses `scripts/embed-site.ts` to generate embeddings and populate the `site_embeddings` table in Supabase.
- By default the script currently indexes `public/llms.txt` and `agents.md`. If you update these files, re-run:

```bash
npx tsx scripts/embed-site.ts
```

- A Husky `pre-push` hook is configured to run the embedding step automatically when tracked source files change; see `.husky/pre-push` for `EMBED_PATHS`.
- To include additional pages in the RAG index, add them to `scripts/embed-site.ts` `filePaths` and update `.husky/pre-push` `EMBED_PATHS`.

---
Last updated: May 2026
